entry.title: Single WF in SAP Data Services
entry.content: Can anyone please explain the sentence that is marked in yellow in the below screenshot?What exactly does different threads mean here?

entry.title: Re: SD downpayment amount should include tax value
entry.content: Dear Emmayoung, I agree with what @yschlaeppi is saying. DP in FI does not include tax. It is just an advance payment. The taxes are calculated on the actual invoice/invoice verification because you have considerable information to tell system to determine taxes. DP then adjusted to the invoice or IV. Thanks Avnish

entry.title: Re: Report that Shows Purchase Orders that have attachmen...
entry.content:
Hello @Marcus_Raisch 

If the attachments were added using DMS then you could use the Manage Documents app for the Document Type involved used to add the attachments to the Purchase Orders to get a list 

 

We have also the released CDS view I_DOCUMENTINFORECORDOBJECTLINK .

For Purchase Orders the BusinessObjectTypeName is  

  • BUS2012 (purchase order)

  • EKPO (purchase order items)

This is documented at the resource Attachments within Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0053

You could use the CDS view I_DOCUMENTINFORECORDOBJECTLINK with BUS2012 or EKPO for LinkedSAPObject to see what DIR are connected to Purchasing Documents and then A_DOCUMENTINFORECORDATTCH to retrieve attachment information

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM support for monitoring Content server

entry.title: Legal Right to use RFC component
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,
I cannot find anything releated to the following topic:

We have a SAP customer, that has various RFC Function Modules provided by a third party company.

Is it legal to use and consume those RFC components? So once a Module has been transported to the customer's SAP he has the right to use this components anytime?
We are not talking about modifiying, Just to use them

I cannot find anythink releated in the SAP Knownledge base.

Many thanks in advance

Thanks regards 

Shubham

entry.title: Re: contract to delivery
entry.content: Hi, thank you for your answer. It really helped me.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Implement Observability in a Full-Stack CAP Application Following SAP BTP Developer’s Guide
entry.content: SAP Cloud Logging is the successor of SAP Application Logging service for SAP BTP. The deprecation of SAP Application Logging service is in preparation and will be announced soon. The detailed timelines for that step will be made available with the upcoming announcement. SAP Cloud Logging can be used as a drop-in replacement.

entry.title: Re: Storage types 020, 021 and 022
entry.content:
Hello @Marcel_Kuijper 

A sequence of storage types during stock placement and stock removal is customized with storage type search strategies - SPRO: Logistics Execution -> Warehouse Management -> Strategies -> Activate Storage Type Search

You can set them up whichever way is required.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA 2022 Maintain Customer Tax (Indirect) Exempt...
entry.content:
Hi Srivijay52,

The answer provided by Vishal Maheshwari is best option for you. Vertex has many SAP add-on Accelerator plus tools to handle Tax automation problems. Flux Builder is one of the tools that provides an option to update customer tax exemption certificates directly from SAP to Vertex automatically. It not only updates the exemption certificate but also provides inbuild a report within SAP about customers’ exemption status in Vertex. Cheery on top, it also allows users to attach the physical certificate copy of the exemption certificate from SAP to Vertex. You can get more detail about the tool in the below link from Vertex:

https://www.vertexinc.com/resources/resource-library/convert-records-exemption-certificate-flux-builder Thanks Avnish

entry.title: Custom Fiori application tile not found in Content Manager
entry.content:
I am facing a peculiar issue, specifically in our Pre-Production environment. There is one singular application for which no results are returned when trying to search for and add its tile in the Content Manager's page designer. It shows up fine on both development and testing environments. I have created a local copy and done all assignments following the other configurations, just in case. The app also is itself listed within the content manager, it just doesn't show up when searching for tiles to add to the page.

Any idea what might be the cause here and if there's a solution available?

Thanks.

entry.title: Show count of selected items in ListPicker
entry.content:
Hi,

Please guide me in implementing below scenario: - 

a. There are four List Picker, State, District, Taluka, Village.

b. Selecting a State will populate respective District, same for Locality and Village.

c.  After selecting aLocality all Village corresponding to State-District-Locality combination should populate in Village listpicker in selected state and should show Village 1, Village 2 + 100 more.

d. Attached screenshot for reference from Web Application.

Regards,

Girish

entry.title: Re: Unable to determine source storage bin during repleni...
entry.content:
Hi 

As per my understanding, source bin determination is a part of stock removal startegy. you need see and check again. Check source bin status it should not be blocked. 

Check setting from scratch it will help you

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: Profit center in settlement document in CCM
entry.content:
Hello,

Please refer to OSS note 1532865 - FAQ: Profit center in billing document. It is standard behavior to have the system clear out the profit center at time of billing for intercompany transactions. This is why SAP has provided another field in VBAP called "PCTRF" which can be filled with a PCA substitution. You should fix the missing profit center at time of order creation and invoice posting. It is also possible to use transfer manager event 65 - Default data item document creation to default the missing profit center as last resort.

entry.title: Re: Create New Production Connector Error
entry.content:
Hi , 

1. check the user role as listed here 

https://help.sap.com/docs/PRODUCTION_CONNECTOR/4e745bf0056e451c85002c21a51d2ebc/59dccc1c44f24cc789819f12d2bc19dc.html?version=2.1

2. Check if the user authorization is maintained inside production connector with specific userid (email). 

1. Open control center in admin mode .

2. Navigate to Cloud integration >> User configuration 

3. Add your DM user details (userid/email)  and assign required roles as below

Hope this helps.

Thanks

Rahul

Thanks

Rahul

entry.title: Fiori Apps for Data Volume Management
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

 

Can you please share Fiori Apps for Data Volume Management?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Apps for Data Volume Management
entry.content:
Fiori Apps for Data Volume Management are designed to help you manage and monitor the volume of data in your SAP systems. They provide a user-friendly interface and a variety of tools to help you analyze data volume, identify potential issues, and take corrective action.

Here are some of the key Fiori Apps for Data Volume Management:

1. Data Volume Management Cockpit: This app provides an overview of the data volume situation in your system. It allows you to monitor data growth, identify large tables, and analyze data distribution.

2. Data Volume Management Analytics: This app provides detailed analytics on data volume, including historical data and forecasts. It can help you identify trends and potential issues.

3. Data Volume Management Work Center: This app provides a central location for all data volume management tasks. It allows you to manage and monitor data volume, perform data aging, and execute data volume reduction measures. Please refer to the following KBA for a screenshot of the apps available in Data Volume Management WorkCenter: 3522671 - Fiori Apps for Data Volume Management https://me.sap.com/notes/3522671

Please note that the availability of these apps may depend on your SAP version and the specific Fiori Apps package you have installed.

For more detailed information, I would recommend visiting the SAP Fiori Apps Library (https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/), where you can find detailed descriptions and technical information for each app.

 

In addition to the Fiori Apps, you might also find the Data Volume Management Cloud App useful. This app provides a comprehensive solution for managing your data volume in a more efficient and effective manner.
For more detailed information about this app, please refer to the following documentation:

2868449 - Data Volume Management App - FAQ - SAP for Me

2716655 - How To Use the Data Volume Management APP - Step by Step guide - SAP for Me

These documents provide a step-by-step guide on how to use the app, as well as answers to frequently asked questions.

entry.title: Re: How To reverse the Stock UPLOADED UNDER /SCWM/ISU
entry.content: Check Queue in SMQ2 it is clear

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – September 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

As we wrap up another exciting month, we wanted to take a moment to share some of the latest updates with you. With September coming to a close, we are getting ready for two major events in the SAP Community - Devtoberfest and TechEd. For more information, be sure to read the rest of this release information.

 

Support SAP S/4HANA Cloud Service Integration for Data Model and App Logic

You can use our generative AI assistant Joule in SAP Build Code to help you to write code quickly and easily by providing natural language prompts. As you know, we are constantly listening to your feedback to make our tools even better. One request we got was to enable AI support for external service scenarios. We are excited to tell you that it is now possible to use Joule to generate code for your integrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud services.

You start by creating your full-stack application, importing an external SAP S/4HANA Cloud service, and defining the data model and services. (With Joule, you can easily create your data model and services.)

Data Model and Service creation with the help of Joule

With our new feature, it is now possible to use Joule to enhance the data model you created before: You can add associations between the external entity and local entities, and you can expose the external entity to a local service via a projection.

Using Joule to add an association between the entity Books and the external entity A_BusinessPartner

We've also added support for custom logic generation to read the associated external SAP S/4HANA Cloud entity and to manipulate the exposed external entity.

Using Joule to add a custom logic reading data from the external entity A_BusinessPartner

 

SQL Notebooks for SAP HANA

SAP Build Code offers notebook functionality, similar to the well-known Jupyter notebooks. Since Q3 2024 the SAP HANA tools in SAP Build Code offer a notebook experience for executing SQL statements on an SAP HANA Cloud database.

To get started, create an SAP HANA dev space. Inside the dev space, create a file with the .notebook extension and open it. The file will be opened with the default notebook editor. Next, create a code block. The default code block type should be “SAP HANA Connection Management”. This code block type allows opening, closing, and selecting a database connection. Typing ‘#’ opens the value help with the available commands.

Choose the “List database connections” Command

Choose the “List database connections” command and execute the code block. A list of the available database connections from the SAP HANA Database Explorer view will be shown. You can choose a database connection to use for the notebook by clicking on its name in the list. The selected database will be highlighted with a different background color.

Select Database Connection

Now you can create another code block for executing SQL statements. Be sure to change the code block type to “SAP HANA SQL” by clicking on the “SAP HANA Connection Management” indicator in the bottom right corner and choosing “SAP HANA SQL” from the subsequent dialog.

Execute SQL Queries

Add one or more SQL statements and execute the code block. The results will be shown below the code block.

Add SQL Statements and Execute the Code Block

You can add more code and markdown blocks as needed.

 

Devtoberfest and TechEd

Get ready for an exciting journey of learning and collaboration with Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd Virtual!

From September 23rd to October 25th, 2024, join us for Devtoberfest, a celebration of coding and collaboration within the SAP Community. This virtual event is designed to prepare you for the highly anticipated SAP TechEd. My colleague Ravi summarizes all you need to know about SAP Build Code at Devtoberfest. For further information, read the Devtoberfest 2024 Welcome blog post and join the Devtoberfest group in SAP Community to take part in this great event!

And don't forget to mark your calendar for SAP TechEd Virtual on October 8-9, 2024, your gateway to the latest innovations and advancements in SAP technologies! The session catalog is now live, featuring a wide range of topics across various tracks, including the Application Development and Automation and the Data and Analytics tracks, where you'll find dedicated sessions on SAP Build Code. You are looking for a summary where to find SAP Build Code at SAP TechEd? Then I recommend to read this blog post.

Don't miss these opportunities to enhance your technical expertise and connect with the SAP community.

 

Let Us Hear Your Voice!

We prepared a short survey, focused on improving the product to better fit your needs. By participating, you can help us improve SAP Build Code and SAP Business Application Studio. It should only take 5 minutes to complete. Please take the survey now.

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: Re: S_ALR_87013047 with matnr more than 18 digits.
entry.content:
I can't find any notes of changing an SETVALMIN/SETVALMAX.
And when i try to call a transaction 
At once as 'AT SELECTION SCREEN OUTPUT' block is started the variable '$7E-MATN' initializes with char24, it cuts off few last characters of the material i have entered at screen.
The type of $7E-MATN'  is KKB_MATNR(char40). And at GR53 there is no any mention of matnr.

entry.title: Selecting hierarchy for Calendar Year/Month dimension
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

In Edit section of Import job when I try to select No hierarchy for Calendar Year/Month dimension it's not possible. Does it have to do something with query settings on BW or with changes since the last SAC update? Colleagues checked settings on BW query, but nothing has been changed since the query was made. 

Kind regards,

Marija

entry.title: Multiple tax condition on a Single Line of Order/billing
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have a business situation that requests me to use handling and freight as condition types in the SD pricing procedure. We are adding freight and handling as condition type on the first line of the order/billing line item.

I also have a business requirement to calculate tax on freight and handling separately from the product price in line 1 of the order/billing.

As per standard SAP, only one tax condition is allowed to be assigned per line. How can I calculate different taxes on product vs Freight assuming both product price and freight charges are on line 1 of the order/billing?

Thanks

Avnish

entry.title: Acknowledgment for PO item contains date variance - turn off
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

is in Public Cloud possible to turn of the error: Acknowledgment for PO item contains date variance when we are creating Inbound Delivery from PO?

Thank you

#SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Document Integration with SharePoint
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM 

Hi everyone,

I'm looking for feedback from anyone who has experience using document integration with SharePoint for storing PDFs or Word documents.

In scenarios where we have multiple projects within the same landscape, how can we manage modifications to solution design or business process documents across these projects? Typically, in Solution Manager's Soldoc, we have options for release and versioning to track these details.

Thanks for your insights!

entry.title: Add custom fields in header area of Manage Purchase Requisition Professional Fiori App
entry.content:
Hi,

I have requirement to add few custom fields in Header area of Manage Purchase requisition professional Fiori app for on premise S/4 Hana 2020.

There is NO business context available to add custom fields at header area using Custom Fields and Logic (CFL) Fiori app. This is ruled out.

Other alternative is extending standard Manage Purchase Requisition Professional Fiori app in BAS or VS Code , but BAS and VS code is not allowing for controller replacement logic for this app. I think this is also ruled out.

Other alternative is creating Extension Project (Manage PR Professional) in WebIDE, while creating extension project I am getting the error as "Extending Smart applications is currently is not supported". 

other alternative is, I have followed this blog Add New Fields To "S/4HANA Manage Purchase Requisi... - SAP Community, but with this I can see the custom fields header area in read only. I am unable to make it as editable. I tried to make these custom field as editable by setting @ObjectModel.readOnly: false. with this I am getting error as "Error related to element 'ZZXXXXX' of entity 'C_PURCHASEREQNHEADER(CDS)': The element ' 1" and Manage Purchase Requisition professional app is not loading.

Could you please let me know how this can be achieved. i,e. Add custom fields in the header area of Manage Purchase Requisition Professional Fiori app

Regards

Rajendar

entry.title: SAP TM: Suppler Invoice Create (B2B) Service not shown in System
entry.content:
Dear experts,

my goal is to implement the Carrier Invoice Interface for TM (also with Dispute Management integration): Supplier Invoice - Create (B2B) | SAP Help Portal. 

In the link above, follwing namespace is mentioned: http://sap.com/xi/TMS/Global

But i cant find that Service with that namespace in SPROXY Transaction. All i find is the InvoiceRequest_In with Namespace "http://sap.com/xi/EDI/Buyer".

Why is that? Should i try the http://sap.com/xi/EDI/Buyer InvoiceRequest_In?

entry.title: Re: Variant Table - Line not valid
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Could you provide more context to identify the problem (e.g., which product, product version, and table you are working with)? Thank you very much!

I'm going to find an expert who can help you. Of course, creating a ticket is more confidential if you do not want to disclose further information here.

Best Regards,

Florian

entry.title: Can I set the ItemCustomerContractReference in a sales order via ABSL
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

maybe you can help me with that issue. I'm working currently on a solution, that create's new sales order items from a custom BO. The request is also to set a CustomerContractReference in those items. I understand, that the Association in the sales order has set the public write access to "no". Though I hoped to find a workaround.

I tried to set a BTDR in the CustomerContract, which returns an error

I tried to use the ItemAccountingCodingBlockDistribution. I can create that association in a sales order (because probably it is not used in sales orders?)

Does anyone have had the same problem or another idea how to change the customer-contract reference in a sales order?

 

Best regards

and thanks in advance

Niko

entry.title: Re: SD downpayment amount should include tax value
entry.content: @yschlaeppi I agree that DP request should not post with sales-tax booking. I think it is the title I give here not exact. what I really want is when DP request post, the value should be a gross amount, because our customer pay the down-payments as gross amount not net amount. if I directly post DP request with net amount, the latter actual payment from customer will be inconsistent. so what I really want to is how to post the DP amount with Gross amount~

entry.title: Re: S_ALR_87013047 with matnr more than 18 digits.
entry.content:
Both data elements use the SETVAL domain, AFAIK this domain should have been converted to 40 when long item numbers were activated.

  • Is the conversion recent
  • Which system version/type ECC vs HANA, on promise vs cloud

Consider raising an incident (a case nowadays)

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to switch my CDS view (odata v2) for POs to OData v4?
Re: SAP Cloud ALM support for monitoring Content server
entry.content:
@MarcinOzdzinski , thank you for your response

 

FYI, I found the following interesting notes:

1722076 - The Future of SAP MaxDB 

1178367 - SAP MaxDB: End of Support Dates 

719971 - SAP Content Server release strategy 

Note 719971 his the following interesting bits:

* SAP Content Server 7.5 (and higher) can be used with any application release, including Business Suite 7, ECC, S/4HANA, etc.

* SAP Content Server shares the same maintenance dates and strategy as SAP Business Suite 7 and SAP S/4HANA

* After the End of Maintenance of SAP MaxDB (see SAP Note 1178367 - SAP MaxDB: End of Support Dates), SAP Content Server may only be used to store documents in the file system. There are no plans to support other database systems for storage in the future

Thank you for highlighting this!

entry.title: BTE_01: Account xxxxxxxx can't be posted with affiliation 'A' (trading partner XXXXXX)

User trying to create document with PO in MIRO and getting error as BTE_01: Account xxxxxxxx can't be posted with affiliation 'A' (trading partner XXXXXX).

Praveen R

entry.title: SAP Enable Now for Fieldglass
entry.content:
Dear,
One of our customers wants to implement SAP Enable Now in their SAP Fieldgladd tenant. I believe there is a kind of package available. Can you please inform me if this is still the case? Every additional information would be welcome.

entry.title: No commitment item entered in item 00001 9000 130800
entry.content:
Hi All,

i have configured Funds management module in PSM. posted the PR,PO and GRN entry. In all the three process budget check is happening. while posting MIRO entry i am having the following error.But i am able to post vendor invoice thru-FB60.no commitment error.

This issue is commitment item is not deriving for vendor Recon. GL- 130800. But i have assigned the commitment item-5000( with FT-60 and item category-30) in the GL master, also in FMDERIVER - cost element to commitment item and Business transaction - RMWR to commitment item. Still i am getting the error as follows.

No commitment item entered in item 00001 9000 130800

Message no. FI311

Diagnosis

The posted document contains at least one company code in which Funds Management, Cash Budget Management, or Project Cash Management is active. Therefore you must enter a commitment item in all involved company codes.

 

Procedure

Enter a commitment item.

If you cannot enter a commitment item, check with your system administrator that your account assignment derivation is adjusted so that the system can automatically derive a commitment item from another account assignment, or from the G/L or cost element.

Request our members please guide me where i am going wrong.

Thanks

NKM

entry.title: Re: SD downpayment amount should include tax value

entry.title: Re: Transporting Fiori Apps in "My Home" space
entry.content: were you able to use the program advised in that note? does it only apply to "Spaces & Pages" or also to the classic Home page group? We tried based on the latter, and it hasn't worked, wondering if we're missing anything?

entry.title: Re: Opa5 FileUploader
entry.content:
Hi,

You can use the following method: fireChange.

In your case, it would look like:

 

 

return this.waitFor({
    id: "fileUploader", 
    controlType: "sap.ui.unified.FileUploader", 
    viewName: sViewName, 
    action: function (oFileUploader) {
        var data = <myData>;
        var blob = new Blob([data], { type: "text/plain" });
        var file = new Blob([blob], "data.txt", { type: "text/plain" });
        fileUploader.fireChange({ newValue: "data.txt", files: [file] });
    }
});

entry.title: What's the best way to monitor the progress of an iFlow using a PowerShell script?
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I am attempting to trigger an iFlow using an HTTP endpoint from a PowerShell script. Since it is a long-running flow, I would like to incorporate a periodic monitoring feature within the script itself.

Is there a monitoring API available that can facilitate this?

Thanks,

Himanshu

entry.title: Re: How to generate seperate spool for each customer in F.27 - Periodic account statements
entry.content:
I have created a spool that customer receives a statement of account every week using FBL5N

However, the Open at Key Date stays as the original and will not use current date when emailing to customer.  How can I get the date to change to a live date rather than using the old date and old data 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: is there any APIs are available for HANA to Park the invoice?
entry.content: What application are you using? HANA itself is the database underneath the application. A task like "parking an invoice" would be performed through the application, not directly at the database level.

entry.title: Re: Unable to determine source storage bin during repleni...
entry.content: Also make sure there is inventory to pick up from in RPPK and/or RHRK

entry.title: Re: We did it... SAP confirmed it is NIST CSF Tier 3
entry.content:
This is such an important achievement! Implementing the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) is crucial for SAP as it provides a structured approach to managing cybersecurity risks, enhancing security posture, and ensuring compliance with regulatory standards. By following NIST CSF, companies can proactively identify and mitigate threats, improve incident response and recovery, and build customer trust by demonstrating a strong commitment to security. It is great to see that we are better positioned to supports scalability, secure software development, and fosters a culture of cybersecurity awareness across the organization. It also ensures resilience, minimizing downtime and operational disruptions in the event of an attack, while helping meet legal and regulatory requirements.

All of this becomes even more important for our customers given that our aim is for them to adopt our software solutions in the cloud and also provide solutions supported by AI.

entry.title: Re: Can you tell me anyone what is the solution of this e...
entry.content:
Hi Shubham, the error is reflecting that tax account key RA2 is not defined.

Request if you can check the Tax Code that you have leveraged in the posting does have any Account key RA2 in it and is GL Account defined for it.
Go to FTXP and verify the tax code - does it has any account key RA2 on it?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to generate seperate spool for each customer in F.27 - Periodic account statements
entry.content: This old thread was related to F.27 not FBL5N, better ask a new question.

entry.title: Re: Proof Of Delivery
entry.content:
Hello @Vivek_Savant 

I don't think you need to do anything else - the system data correctly reflects the business situation:

  1. 10 units have been issued and posted to cost of good sold; that is what has happened in the warehouse
  2. the customer has confirmed receipt of 8 units, i.e. that 2 units have vanished into thin air - maybe they have been lost or stolen during transit
  3. therefore you can only invoice 8 units

Unless, the missing 2 units are found, you don't need to do anything else.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: I need to add an additional us gaap ledger during a move ECC to a S/4HANA private cloud
entry.content:
Dear community members,

A customer of mine is about to move from its ECC system to a S/4HANA Private Cloud, and asked me if it is possible to add an US GaaP ledger in addition of its local ledger.

Anyone already faced this issue ?

Thanks for support 

BR

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: My buddy Sully

entry.title: License Consumption History in Customer Insights Dashboard
entry.content:
The contractual license consumption is a crucial part for gaining a comprehensive overview of your usage, adoption, and consumption patterns. Additionally to the current overview of the License Consumption a new section dedicated to License Consumption history has been introduced in Customer Insights reporting dashboard.

Accessing the License Consumption Section

You can access the section License Consumption in  ‘Customer Insights‘ dashboard  from the Entry page using the relevant 'Summary' card or by navigating to the “License Consumption” section in the Tab Menu. This section is divided into two tabs: one for current consumption and a new one for consumption history.

Understanding License Consumption History

The History of the License Consumption displays your contractual license consumption for the selected period for all measured license metrics. All metrics are categorized based on their consumption levels, allowing you to easily identify over-consumption or under-consumption: 

  • Potential Over-Consumption: Usage exceeds 100% of subscriptions.
  • Potential Under-Consumption: Low consumption (< 20%).
  • Regular License Consumption: Consumption between 20% and 100%.
  • Measured Consumption Without Subscriptions.

A monthly graph illustrates the total number of license metrics with specific consumption characteristics, enabling you to quickly identify trends across all subscriptions. This helps you determine whether regular consumption is growing or if low consumption is increasing, indicating a need for improvement.

 

Customization and Detailed Analysis

You can filter the data by time period or select a specific Solution Area to view consumption trends for particular product groups. Clicking on a segment or line in the chart displays all metrics belonging to the selected Consumption Intensity Type for the chosen month, providing detailed information in a table format.

The table includes additional details such as the number of subscriptions for each metric, links to metric descriptions and documentation (Customer Info Sheet), and consumption measurements. You can customize the table by adding more columns using the customization wheel icon. The report date indicates when the last updated consumption data was received.

Special Cases and Visualization Options

A special case comes up when a metric is being metered per Entitlement Set ID. In this case the same license metric would appear several times, once for each entitlement set and it can have different consumption intensity for different entitlement sets. If you use the filter to display such special metrics, please note that the line diagram might not be the best graph layout to use. The limitation here is that in case the same number of metrics has different consumption intensity, the different lines will overlap, and you only are able to see the line in front but not the line in the back.

For metrics metered per Entitlement Set ID, the same license metric may appear multiple times with different consumption intensities. In such cases, the line diagram might not be the best visualization due to overlapping lines. Switching to a stacked column chart can provide a clearer view of all values. You can easily change the graph layout to e.g. stacked column chart by clicking on the graph icon in the upper right corner.

Example:

Line Chart: Only 4 regular consumption metrics are visible for September in the graph.

Stacked Column Chart: Both 4 low and 4 regular consumption metrics are displayed.

Utilizing the License History Trend 

Use the new License History trend to overlook your overall consumption, compare different product groups and solutions and analyze single metrics history.

Use the License History trend to monitor overall consumption, compare different product groups and solutions, and analyse individual metrics history.

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the SAP Support Portal / Customer Insights.

Your feedback is valuable to us. Please use the ‘Feedback’ slider on the right edge of the page of the application to share with us your feedback on this new tool and help us to improve the reporting functionality in SAP for Me.

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the SAP Support Portal / Customer Insights.

SAP for Me is made for you!

Related blogs: SAP for Me goes reporting, License consumption in Customer Insights  or all related blogs to ‘Customer Insights’ reporting dashboard.

entry.title: Discussion forum closes soon!
entry.content:
Dear course participants,

Thank you for learning with us and posting your course related questions on this forum. 

We look forward to your posts, if you would like to address them before the course forum engagement ends on September 30, 2024. Our course experts are available until September 30, 2024 to answer your questions.  

Meanwhile, happy learning on our course " Modernizing Integration with SAP Integration Suite" and good luck with your quiz to earn your record of achievement!

Best regards,
Jayanthi
SAP Learning team

entry.title: Re: Legal Right to use RFC component
entry.content:
Hi @Sandra_Rossi , I'm referring to sap RFC functions itselves, not the connectors.

So, to be more precise, can i legally use an already installed third party RFC i.e. named: /XXX/GET_MY_CUSTOMERS ?

entry.title: Re: Building custom Excel view in SAPUI5 : Technical Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @TrusPatel ,
thank you for your blog post.

Unfortunately there are a few things in there that are not best practie:
Most importantly, your usage of third party libraries. It´s not just really bad practice to load a external js file from a CDN, it is also a very old version of this library. The current version is "0.20.3".
If you would have researched just a little bit you would have seen that.
They even have a section in their documentation: https://docs.sheetjs.com/docs/getting-started/installation/amd#sap-ui5
The best way is use ui5-tooling-modules: https://www.npmjs.com/package/ui5-tooling-modules
Your way of "integration" would not work in any Fiori Launchpad.

Why do you use that in "onInit"?

 

that = this;

 

 Why do you mix Hungarian notation and non Hungarian notation?
Why do you use "var"? 
Why do you use all the controls like sap.m.Table directly?

new sap.m.Table

 You switch between namespaces between sample files, why?

By large, this is a very bad example of code. Please never use this code in a productive.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Greetings, Builders! We’re pleased to bring you the Q3 Edition of our What’s New in SAP Build series. In this edition, we’re covering product-specific updates for:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Code

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud: The extensibility wizard is now available with the 2408 edition of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. This makes the creation of side-by-side extensions even more streamlined and allows you to seamlessly navigate to SAP Build from your Cloud ERP. This first release enables custom process creation, with more options from SAP Build to become available this year. See more in the blog.

Scheduled trigger: Now you can schedule process automations based on user-defined time intervals and dates at runtime. This allows you to enable system-centric and time-based process automation use cases, such as working and nonworking days in creating your automation scenarios to avoid resource mismatches.

Additional Outcomes in Forms and UI5 Support for Tasks: This enables citizen developers to configure additional outcome actions in the process to let approvers request rework in case of missing or wrong information in approval forms. You can also start filling out multiple forms efficiently through a guided experience.

Advanced Event Project Creation with Manual Specification Upload: This allows users to upload business event specifications for the creation of an event project in the lobby. The new feature adds support for business events across lines of business (LOBs) and custom events, including third-party events that are supported at runtime using SAP Event Mesh.

Process Visibility enhancements: These include actionable insights, instance tracking for requestors and additional monitoring capabilities via CALM. Now you can turn insights into meaningful outcomes and enable the triggering of escalation flows based on visibility insights, to directly react and resolve critical situations. This enables auditable and governed escalation resolution, instead of having disconnected communication through different channels.

More on SAP Build Process Automation:

  • See the What's New Viewer for more updates, and improvements.
  • See SAP Road Map Explorer for more upcoming features and focus areas.

SAP Build Apps

Release management and project lifecycle management: It’s now possible to release and transport projects across different tenants using SAP Cloud Transport Management System. You can transport project versions between subaccounts while isolating development, testing, and productive deployment of SAP Build Apps applications to their own landscapes. To get started, see this blog post.

Copying pages within one project: This feature enables users to copy and duplicate a page within a project in SAP Build Apps – a simple, yet powerful, add-on to further speed up development.

New Build Service: We recently launched a new and improved experience for deploying application packages. The build service includes managing image assets, iOS usage strings, mobile service enablement, package creation, and configuration management. The updated UI provides a much smoother experience for developers at the deployment phase, ensuring that every requirement is met and that your apps are ready to publish. Try it out today!

  • Learn more about what’s coming up on the SAP Build Apps Roadmap.
  • Also check out Ludo Noens’ series on Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps – Creating a CRUD Application

SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, and SAP Task Center

SAP Build Work Zone, both editions

HTML5 Business Solution as a Content Provider: With the latest update in SAP Build Work Zone, you can now expose BTP-based applications as Work Zone content, like roles, spaces, and pages, in the 'Common Data Model' (CDM) format. As a result, you can easily configure and enable access to BTP applications from a Work Zone instance, even across subaccounts. See more in the Help Portal and this blog post.

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Define Sort Order of pages and spaces: Administrators can now configure how spaces and pages are sorted on the menu bar of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. This feature is only applicable if you're working in the new experience view mode for spaces and pages. See more in the Help Portal.

Import and Export of Pages and Spaces: Content manager tools will be enhanced to enable administrators to export and import custom (locally created) spaces and pages between landscapes.

SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition

Integrated guided experience across SAP Build Work Zone and Process Automation*: Guided processes are now available as an artifact in SAP Build Process Automation. In addition to the previously available “no template” wizards, it is now possible to create guided, process-based wizards in SAP Build Work Zone.

*Note: This will become GA in October 2024. 

SAP Start

Directly navigate to product home pages of integrated cloud solutions: The SAP Start site offers a new product section to easily see and navigate to the product home pages of the integrated solutions. This enables transparency of available cloud solutions for the current user, and quick and easy navigation to launch the product home page of the desired solution.

SAP Task Center

ITCE partner page: SAP Task Center now allows connections with third-party task providers. The first solution to leverage this capability is TaskConnect, developed by ITCE in collaboration with SAP architects and technical consultants. The application will soon be available on the SAP Store as well, offering a comprehensive configuration guide, installation manuals and BPMN diagrams. For more information about TaskConnect, see here.

More on SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start and SAP Task Center:

  • Check out the roadmaps for SAP Build Work Zone standard & advanced edition and SAP Start / SAP Task Center
  • What's New Viewers: Standard edition / Advanced edition / SAP Start / SAP Task Center 

SAP Build Code

New AI Features: In SAP Build Code, you can now use the Joule copilot, our generative AI assistant, to write code by providing natural language prompts. For a detailed explanation of Joule, please read this article. In the last months we added many new features to this capability including the following highlights:

  • Support SAP S/4HANA Cloud Service Integration for Data Model and App Logic: It is now possible to use Joule to generate code for your integrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud services.
  • Major UX Update of Joule in SAP Build Code: With the latest UX update of Joule within SAP Build Code we enhanced the usability, streamlined workflows, and created a more intuitive interface, boosting productivity and satisfaction.
  • Generative AI support for productivity tools: We now allow you to use Joule directly from the storyboard.
  • Use Joule to enhance your already existing data models and services: It is now possible to modify already existing data model and service entities using a prompt.

Why SAP Build Code: Check out this blog post by Product Manager Rupa Maity describing why to move to SAP Build Code.

Stay updated on the latest Build Code innovations: Do you want to stay informed about the latest product updates for SAP Build Code? Follow our tag on SAP Community and subscribe to our SAP Build Code Newsletter. 

  • See the latest update product updates from September 2024 for SAP Build Code.
  • Learn more on the SAP Build Code Community.

--- 

💡Please note this blog post provides a highlight of the latest features and enhancements. 

  • For comprehensive list of all updates, please refer to our Help Portal What’s New in SAP Build.
  • Join the SAP Builders group for product resources and community questions.
  • Devtoberfest is here! 🥨 Don't miss our SAP Build-themed sessions and more.
  • ...and that means SAP TechEd is right around the corner! Explore SAP Build at TechEd 2024.

Which feature are you most excited to test out? Let us know in the comments!

entry.title: Re: Custom Fiori application tile not found in Content Ma...

entry.title: Changes in the Communication of the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil team has released updates on the communication of the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy (NF3-e) solution with SEFAZ.

NF3-e Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version

This update complies with the SEFAZ NF3-e Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version, which announced the discontinuation of the asynchronous authorization service, also known as the batch submission service. The communication between the SAP service and SEFAZ now is synchronous.

This communication change has been updated in the outbound invoicing option for Brazil service, and it is not necessary to implement SAP Notes in your backend system. For official communication on this update, read the SAP Note 3509349.

Check out the latest updates and features in our What's New session. And for a step-by-step guide on how to use the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy (NF3-e) solution, refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • Integration > NF3-e Specifics
  • Using Outbound Nota Fiscal > NF3-e Specifics

Related blog posts:

  • Usage Analytics Dashboard
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2478289.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR-OU (Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy, NF3-e)
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT (Nota Fiscal Eletrônica Outbound)

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content. You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

Tags: brazil, brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, electronic nota fiscal, outbound, nf3-e, nf-e, electric energy, energia elétrica, is-u, nota técnica 2024.002, 2024/002, 2024.002

You can check with your Emarsys CSM and they will be able to help you with the application.

entry.title: SAP Service Cloud Workflow field update use string in the formula

Is it possible to have a blank space between it? 
I tried with " or ' but it is not working, it is showing a syntax error... 

Does somebody have a tip?

Thanks a lot!

Deborah

entry.title: SAP Product Inspiration Series for Industries & Intelligent CX (Wave #4)
entry.content:
Hi everyone! Today I'm more than excited to let you know we go full steam ahead with our Product Inspiration Video Series into the 2nd half of the year and covering the latest innovations of SAP Intelligent CX along with S/4HANA, Business AI, Joule, BTP and industry-tailored solutions. Please find the latest deep dive videos from our product teams below.

 

1. Mediafly AI Coaching & Enablement for SAP Sales Cloud & Service Cloud Version 2

Ben Turner, Director Product Management at Mediafly provides an overview on AI Coaching and Enablement for SAP Customer Experience. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the strategic direction of Mediafly in building this solution with latest integrations to SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 as well as our overall innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:26 - Why Mediafly and SAP Sales and Service Cloud?
02:02 - How can customers benefits from this solution?
03:46 - What is your overall direction in terms of AI in Mediafly and SAP?
05:32 - Start of the Demo
06:55 - Demo: Mediafly Content Workspace in the Account View
08:22 - Demo: Mediafly Recommended Content for each Account
10:05 - Demo: Mediafly Call Coaching
11:19 - Demo: Mediafly Content Shares
12:14 - Demo: Mediafly Value Tools
13:42 - Demo: Mediafly Content Workspace cntd.
14:38 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

2. SAP Service Cloud for Utilities in Action

Tina Kuo from the Product Management at SAP provides an overview on latest utilities scenarios in SAP Service Cloud Version 2. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about customer service processes for utilities, how these are deeply connected to SAP S/4HANA as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio from SAP.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:59 - Why Service Scenarios tailored to Utility Companies?
02:11 - Which Utility Scenarios are available with SAP Service Cloud Version 2?
04:28 - How can Utilities adopt SAP Service Cloud Version 2?
05:19 - Start of the Demo
05:32 - Demo: Manage Incoming Calls with Agent Desktop
05:60 - Demo: Overview on Customer Premises, Contract Accounts, Interactions etc.
07:31 - Demo: Contract Account Overview
11:07 - Demo: Contract Account - Financial Metrics, Open Items, Invoices, Payments
12:11 - Demo: Contract Account - Collections, Dunning, Write Offs, Correspondence, Returns
12:24 - Demo: Contract Account - Payment Arrangement, Promise to Pay, Payment Plans, Installment Plans
12:35 - Demo: Contract Account - Security Deposit and Exceptions
13:20 - Demo: Premise - Overview and Service Status
14:58 - Demo: Premise - Change Move-Out Data
15:15 - Demo: Create new Service Case for Premise
15:46 - Demo: Get Latest Insights on Services Consumption
16:30 - Demo: Manage Customers assigned to Service and Installation ID
17:00 - Demo: Start new Move-In Process
17:14 - Demo: Move-In Process - Customer and Premise
18:08 - Demo: Move-In Process - Select Services
18:21 - Demo: Move-In Process - Contract Account and Billing
19:32 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

3. SAP Enterprise Service Management for Finance

Akshay Shripathi, Senior Product Manager SAP Enterprise Service Management at SAP provides an overview of SAP ESM, the new shared service framework for Finance and HR. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the strategic direction and importance of SAP Enterprise Service Management for Finance scenarios as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:52 - Why SAP Enterprise Service Management for Finance?
02:19 - Which Scenarios are supported by SAP Enterprise Service Management?
02:50 - Which Scenarios are Supported?
03:17 - How can customers adopt SAP CX AI Toolkit for Sales and Service?
03:35 - How can Customers Adopt SAP Enterprise Service Management?
03:49 - Which Scenarios are Supported?
04:16 - How can customers adopt SAP CX AI Toolkit for Sales and Service?
04:48 - Which Scenarios are Supported?
05:01 - Start of the Demo
06:50 - Demo: Create Accrual Request in SAP Work Zone
11:13 - Demo: Agent View in SAP ESM
11:36 - Demo: Case Details in SAP ESM
17:33 - Demo: Configure Case Types and Flows
18:50 - Demo: Build Forms in the Form Designer
20:15 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

4. SAP Enterprise Service Management for HR

Akshay Shripathi, Senior Product Manager SAP Enterprise Service Management at SAP provides an overview of SAP ESM, the new shared service framework for Finance and HR. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the strategic direction and importance of SAP Enterprise Service Management for HR scenarios as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:52 - Why SAP Enterprise Service Management for HR?
02:19 - Which scenarios are supported by SAP ESM
03:35 - How can customers adopt SAP ESM?
05:01 - Start of the Demo
08:52 - Demo: Processing Request in SAP Enterprise Service Management
12:01 - Demo: Add Digital Signature via Docusign
13:39 - Demo: Approve Process in SAP SuccessFactors
15:30 - Demo: How to Configure such HR Processes in SAP Enterprise Service Management
18:19 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

5. Intelligent Quote Management in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 with S/4HANA in Action

Guenter Wilmer from the Sales Cloud Product Management at SAP provides an overview on Intelligent Quote Management. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the functional capabilities of Intelligent Quote Management in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio from SAP.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:55 - Why Integrated Quotation Management in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?
03:40 - What are Top Innovations in Sales Quotation Management as part of V2?
05:28 - How can Customer Adopt Quotation Management in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?
06:21 - Start of the Demo
07:32 - Demo: Sales Quotes Worklist
09:33 - Demo: Creation of a new Quote
10:34 - Demo: Add Items from Product Lists
12:52 - Demo: Request External Pricing from SAP S/4HANA
14:24 - Demo: Preview Sales Quote as PDF Document
15:00 - Demo: Submit Sales Quote and Send it to the Customer
16:11 - Demo: Availability-to-Promise (ATP) Check with SAP S/4HANA
17:15 - Demo: Credit Limit Check with SAP S/4HANA
18:10 - Demo: Create Sales Order in SAP S/4HANA
19:22 - Demo: Navigate to the Sales Order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
20:01 - Demo: Configuration of Sales Quotes in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
20:11 - Demo: How to Configure Document Types
21:14 - Demo: How to Configure Item Types
21:26 - Demo: How to Configure Reasons for Progress
21:48 - Demo: How to Configure Party Schema
23:24 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

6. SAP Recommerce in Action

Miguel Valenzuela from the Product Management team at SAP provides an overview on SAP Recommerce and circular business models. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the strategic imperatives of SAP Recommerce, how it complements our SAP Commerce Cloud offering as well as major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio from SAP.

00:00 - Introduction
00:54 - Why SAP Recommerce?
02:12 - Which Innovative Scenarios does SAP Recommerce offer?
03:03 - How can Customer Adopt SAP Recommerce?
04:08 - Start of the Demo
04:24 - Demo: Customer adds Trade-In Items in SAP Recommerce
04:48 - Demo: Search Trade-In Items via the Product Catalog
06:22 - Demo: Search for Another Trade-In Item via the Order History
07:15 - Demo: Customer Confirms Trade-In and Performs a Check-out in SAP Recommerce
08:04 - Demo: Recommerce Manager processes Trade-In Orders in SAP Recommerce
08:35 - Demo: Recommerce Manager confirms that the Order has been received
09:04 - Demo: Process the Check-In of a Particular Item
09:14 - Demo: Enter a Barcode or Serial Number
09:59 - Demo: Verify SKU Submitted by the Customer
10:18 - Demo: Verify the Condition
11:29 - Demo: Choose Process Flow for the Item
12:13 - Demo: Clean Step for Trade-In Items
14:03 - Demo: Repair Step for Trade-In Items
14:53 - Demo: Regrade Step for Trade-In Items
15:20 - Demo: Re-Stock Step for Trade-In Items
16:22 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

7. Mention Me Customer Advocacy Platform for SAP Intelligent CX (link coming soon)

Anca Filip, VP of Product at Mention Me, provides an overview on the Customer Advocacy Platform for SAP Customer Experience. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the strategic direction of Mention Me in building this solution with integration to SAP Commerce Cloud, SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement and SAP Customer Data Platform (CDP) as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:47 - Why Mention Me and SAP CX?
02:22 - How can customers benefit from your solution?
04:33 - What is Mention Me’s roadmap when it comes to AI?
06:47 - Start of the Demo
07:30 - Demo: Mention Me Javascript Tags in SAP Commerce Cloud
12:35 - Demo: Mention Me Advocacy Dashboard & Reporting
15:04 - Demo: Mention Me Customer Profiles in SAP Emarsys
16:11 - Demo: Mention Me Engagement Tactics in SAP Emarsys
17:37 - Demo: Mention Me AI-based Sentiment Analysis and Voice of Customer
18:40 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

8. Frontnow AI Advisor for SAP Commerce Cloud (link coming soon)

Marc Funk, Founder & CEO, togehter with Eva Kalbfell, Senior Product Owner from Frontnow provides an overview on the Frontnow AI Advisor for SAP Commerce Cloud. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about their strategic direction of the Frontnow AI Advisor powered by Generative AI, its integration to SAP Commerce Cloud along with a compelling customer example.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Start
01:08 - Why Frontnow AI Advisor for SAP Commerce Cloud?
02:33 - Which Scenarios does Frontnow AI Advisor offer?
05:55 - What’s the Future Roadmap of Frontnow AI Advisor?
08:45 - Start of the Demo
09:11 - Demo: Frontnow Advisor on the Storefront
11:23 - How to Implement the Frontnow AI Advisor
12:08 - Demo: Setup a New Frontnow AI Advisor
12:47 - Demo: Configure Design and Settings
14:02 - Demo: Define Success Feedback to Further Fine-Tune AI Model
15:05 - Demo: Embed API and Code Snippet on SAP Storefront
16:25 - Architecture of the Frontnow AI Advisor
18:54 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

If you're curious to learn more about Intelligent CX from SAP, please check-out the other deep dive videos:  

Back to the Product Inspiration Overview  |  Wave I  |  Wave II  |  Wave III

Enjoy and get inspired about Intelligent CX from SAP.

entry.content:
Hello Guillaume,

As your influence seems to be still in implementation (status changed on 24.08.2024). May I ask if you found any solution to bypass the issue with the upload? 🙂

 

Kind regards 

Stefan

entry.title: Re: Host a CodeJam in Berlin
entry.content: Thank you for the quick answer. We are fine with a CodeJam in January next year. Looking forward to the preparation and then the session.

entry.title: Job interval impact for transport creation, release and import from SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi, 

according to this page:https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/setting-up-managed-systems?locale=en-US

The data transfer between on-premise SAP system to SAP Cloud ALM, is always the direction that on-premise system PUSH data to Cloud ALM.

Does it mean that, if I follow the default collection interval as defined in /n/SDF/ALM_SETUP, then I need to wait the corresponding minutes for the transports to be created / released / imported in the backend on premise system, after I perform the action in Cloud ALM?

For example, when I create a transport in SAP Cloud ALM, then I need to wait maximum 10 minutes, before the transport can be created in backend on-premise system?

if yes, what's the risk, if I shorten the interval to 1 minute?

I mean, currently in SAP Solution Manager ChaRM (Change and Request Management), it's a syncronous process. When I trigger transport creation in ChaRM, it's created at the same time in the backend SAP system, and I know from ChaRM immediately if the creation is successfully or not.
When it comes to SAP Cloud ALM, if this is an asyncronous process, it's very user unfriendly. Firstly I need to wait up to 10 minutes for the transport to be created, and then another 10 minutes for the status to be reflected in Cloud ALM.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Job interval impact for transport creation, release a...
entry.content:
Yes, the interval of the job for Implementation features has a direct impact on the time it takes to create, release, and import Transport Requests. The default values are set with high numbers, but can be changed to lower numbers if desired. 

The SAP Help Portal documentation for SAP Cloud ALM outlines the following in section Configuring the PUSH Data Provider (just above section Configuration of the Transport Organizer Web UI😞
Note
For your test or productive landscape, set the collection interval to 1 min for these use cases if you want a quicker reaction to your testing of creating transport request, transport of copies, and triggering the deploy in the features. 

SAP Cloud ALM is not an on-premise system, and as such it does not use the same technology to communicate with the on-premise systems. It must use asynchronous communication to trigger actions and receive updates from on-premise systems. 

Reducing the job interval time carries the same risks as scheduling any periodic job at very short intervals. If the jobs have to trigger a lot of tasks or transmit a lot of data back to SAP Cloud ALM, this can consume on-premise system resources and impact performance. If this happens, the configuration can be altered to increase the interval value at small increments (1 minute at a time) to find a balance between on-premise system performance and SAP Cloud ALM update wait times. If the on-premise performance impact is severe and seems like an issue, a Case should be created to investigate in case an issue or bug is involved.

entry.title: Feature Request: Enrichment of attribute names in Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to address the following feature request:

Problem: When working with business objects in Signavio Process Intelligence our users sometimes struggle with relating the attribute names to the actual data in SAP. Some users prefer the naming in "natural language", others prefer the technical names (table and field names). 

Current situation:

1) We are currently maintaining a documentation aside from the actual Intelligence Dashboards where we translate the natural language names to the field names and vice versa. This documentation is not accepted well, as users want to have the information in the tool where they actually work in.

2) For one dashboard we have named the attributes with both: The natural language plus the table and field name. This resolves the issue of having to look things up in a different space, but results in very long column/attribute names and lots of waisted space in the dashboard: 

Feature Request:

We would like to request some sort of rule, that - if applied in the backend - results in splitting the attribute names by a given parameter (eg. a | (pipe symbol)), so that the part before the pipe becomes the actual attribute name, and the part after the name can be retrieved through a mouse-over, wherever the attribute is used (e.g. in the filtering options, in column names when displaying data in tables, etc...):

This would save space in the actual dashboard and still allow for additional context where needed.

Best, Anna

entry.title: Using Contract functionality to give first 5 orders as free
entry.content:
Hi, 

 

We have a special condition type ( price). A material is created to charge this fees to customer. We create a debit note and enter this material to charge customer. The requirement is to make sure that first 5 orders which are created should be free and prices should only be picked from 6th order. Prices are getting picked from VK11 only. Can we use contract functionality for this?

entry.title: Re: User-defined fields order
entry.content:
1. OK, I did it. In my case this is the Item Master Data

2. If I select Form Settings, I can't edit UDFs. I can edit the order in Customitaion Tools -> Settings

3. I don't have the drag and drag option with this method at the UDFs section.

4. OK

5. Every fields are visible

@SonTranprovided a solution, this is how it works for me

entry.title: Re: CAP OData srv module with custom CDS service provider
entry.content: Hi @marcbecker, I have implemented similar approach but I am not able to set custom tags in response like nextLink etc. I am using ResultBuilder.selectedRows. Any doc or example you could give , it will be very helpful. Thanks.
